data.name: Intro
data.value: UncannyPrompt exposes authenticated JSON endpoints for the same core concepts used by the web app: prompts, Projects, folders, tags, variables, sharing, public links, scope, and audit.

data.name: AuthCookie
data.value: Browser calls use the normal signed-in application session.

data.name: AuthApiKey
data.value: Automation can authenticate by sending an API key in the

data.name: AuthBearer
data.value: The same key can also be sent as

data.name: AuthSwagger
data.value: Swagger is available to authenticated users when the host enables it.

data.name: PermissionsDescription
data.value: API requests run as the user who owns the session or key. Tenant roles and explicit shares still decide what the caller can view, edit, share, publish, restore, or audit.

data.name: CatalogDescription
data.value: Create, update, resolve, tag, organize, and delete prompt content through structured endpoints.

data.name: AutomationDescription
data.value: Connect prompt libraries to internal tools, release workflows, support systems, or content pipelines.

data.name: GovernanceDescription
data.value: Use audit, shares, public links, and version endpoints to keep automated usage traceable.

data.name: EndpointsIntro
data.value: The current web API is grouped around these route families.

data.name: DetailsDescription
data.value: This public page explains the idea. The exact examples, Swagger entry point, and API key management are available after sign-in from the Developers area.
